---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}

![](https://via.placeholder.com/200x200/2c3e50/ffffff?text=QL){.rounded-circle style="border-radius: 50%; width: 180px;"}

### Qijia Liao 廖琦佳

PhD Researcher in Marketing  
University of Liverpool Management School

---

**Interests**  
Quantitative Marketing · NLP · Machine Learning · Brand Analytics

---

**Links**  
[{{< fa brands google-scholar >}} Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=s2lwLxMAAAAJ)  
[{{< fa brands github >}} GitHub](https://github.com/qijialiv-cell)

:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="65%"}

## About

I am a PhD student in Marketing at the **University of Liverpool Management School**, where I work on quantitative and computational approaches to marketing problems.

My research sits at the intersection of **machine learning**, **natural language processing**, and **consumer behaviour** — applying modern data science methods to understand how brands, consumers, and markets interact.

---

## Research Interests

- **Computational Marketing** — text-as-data approaches to brand and consumer research  
- **Machine Learning for Marketing** — predictive models, customer analytics, churn prediction  
- **Causal Inference** — identifying marketing effects beyond correlation  
- **NLP** — sentiment analysis, topic modelling, embedding-based representations

---

## News

- **May 2026** — Teaching Analytics system deployed for student feedback collection
- **2026 Spring** — Teaching *Quantitative Marketing Analytics* at UoL Management School
- **Ongoing** — Working on brand activism and influencer ML research projects

:::

:::
